name: UNFCCC 1992 + Paris Agreement 2015 (UN Framework Convention on Climate Change)
summary: The UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) and Paris Agreement is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-UNFCCC-treaty framework adopted by the UN Conference on Environment and Development UNCED in Rio de Janeiro — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-climate-change framework + foundational climate-change framework alongside Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement framework (UNFCCC signed 9 May 1992 in New York + opened for signature 4-14 June 1992 at UNCED Rio Earth Summit + effective 21 March 1994 + Kyoto Protocol signed 11 December 1997 in Kyoto Japan + effective 16 February 2005 + Paris Agreement adopted 12 December 2015 in Paris France at COP21 + effective 4 November 2016 — formal foundational UNFCCC + Paris Agreement treaty framework establishing comprehensive UNFCCC + Paris Agreement organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-climate-change framework + foundational climate-change framework alongside Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Common But Differentiated Responsibilities CBDR principle framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Nationally Determined Contributions NDCs framework via Paris Agreement Article 4 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework via Paris Agreement Article 2 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Trump-administration-Paris-Agreement-withdrawal oscillation 2017-2021-2025 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal COP-Conference-of-the-Parties annual-meeting framework via COP1 1995 Berlin to COP30 2025 Belém Brazil framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Global Stocktake 2023 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Loss and Damage Fund 2023-2024 framework via COP27 + COP28 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Just Transition Work Programme framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Mitigation Work Programme framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CARICOM-led ICJ Climate Change Advisory Opinion 2025 framework via Vanuatu UN Resolution 77/276), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Paris Agreement 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework (Paris Agreement Article 2 framework providing formal Holding the increase in global average temperature to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels and pursuing efforts to limit the temperature increase to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels framework — formal foundational Paris Agreement temperature-goal framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Nationally Determined Contributions NDCs framework (Paris Agreement Article 4 framework providing formal NDCs framework with formal five-year-cycle ratcheting-up framework — formal foundational NDCs framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal NDCs framework with formal bottom-up self-determined-contributions framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Loss and Damage Fund + Climate Finance framework (Loss and Damage Fund established at COP27 Sharm El-Sheikh Egypt 6-20 November 2022 + operationalized at COP28 Dubai UAE 30 November - 13 December 2023 + USD 700+ million pledges framework + USD 100 billion annual climate-finance target adopted 2009 Copenhagen Accord + replaced by USD 300 billion annual NCQG New Collective Quantified Goal adopted COP29 Baku Azerbaijan 11-22 November 2024 framework — formal foundational Loss and Damage Fund + Climate Finance framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Loss and Damage Fund framework). The UNFCCC + Paris Agreement is binding upon 198 UNFCCC member states + 195 Paris Agreement member states as of 2026 — comprehensive universal coverage framework. The framework is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Paris Agreement Just Transition framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal cross-border-climate-displacement-of-families framework. The UNFCCC COP Conference of the Parties + CMA Conference of the Parties serving as the meeting of the Parties to the Paris Agreement + UNFCCC Secretariat + UNFCCC Subsidiary Bodies are the formal UNFCCC bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The UNFCCC + Paris Agreement is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: UNFCCC
statutory_framework.relevance: UN Framework Convention on Climate Change UNFCCC signed 9 May 1992 in New York + opened for signature 4-14 June 1992 at UNCED Rio Earth Summit + effective 21 March 1994 — formal foundational UNFCCC treaty framework establishing comprehensive UNFCCC organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: Kyoto Protocol
statutory_framework.relevance: Kyoto Protocol to the UNFCCC signed 11 December 1997 in Kyoto Japan + effective 16 February 2005 — formal foundational Kyoto Protocol top-down-targets framework.

statutory_framework.title: Paris Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: Paris Agreement adopted 12 December 2015 in Paris France at COP21 + effective 4 November 2016 — formal foundational Paris Agreement bottom-up-NDCs framework.

statutory_framework.title: Paris Agreement Article 2
statutory_framework.relevance: Paris Agreement Article 2 framework on 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal — formal foundational Paris Agreement temperature-goal framework.

statutory_framework.title: Paris Agreement Article 4
statutory_framework.relevance: Paris Agreement Article 4 framework on Nationally Determined Contributions NDCs — formal foundational NDCs framework.

statutory_framework.title: Loss and Damage Fund
statutory_framework.relevance: Loss and Damage Fund established at COP27 Sharm El-Sheikh Egypt 6-20 November 2022 + operationalized at COP28 Dubai UAE 30 November - 13 December 2023 — formal foundational Loss and Damage Fund framework.

apex_courts.name: UNFCCC COP Conference of the Parties
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for UNFCCC.

apex_courts.name: CMA Conference of the Parties serving as the meeting of the Parties to the Paris Agreement
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for Paris Agreement.

apex_courts.name: UNFCCC Secretariat
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative body for UNFCCC + Paris Agreement.

apex_courts.name: UNFCCC Subsidiary Body for Scientific and Technological Advice (SBSTA)

professional_regulators.name: UNFCCC Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: UNFCCC Convention + Paris Agreement implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: UNFCCC-related communications operate via state-state framework via UNFCCC COP + CMA + Subsidiary Bodies; individual communications not formally accepted under UNFCCC framework.

key_developments.title: IPCC Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
key_developments.description: IPCC Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change established 1988 by WMO + UNEP — predecessor framework to UNFCCC.

key_developments.title: UNFCCC adopted at UNCED Rio Earth Summit
key_developments.description: UNFCCC signed 9 May 1992 in New York + opened for signature 4-14 June 1992 at UNCED Rio Earth Summit — formal foundational UNFCCC treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: UNFCCC enters force
key_developments.description: UNFCCC entered force 21 March 1994 — formal foundational UNFCCC treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: COP1 Berlin
key_developments.description: COP1 held 28 March - 7 April 1995 in Berlin Germany — formal first UNFCCC Conference of the Parties framework operational.

key_developments.title: Kyoto Protocol adopted
key_developments.description: Kyoto Protocol to the UNFCCC adopted 11 December 1997 in Kyoto Japan — formal foundational Kyoto Protocol top-down-targets framework adopted.

key_developments.title: Kyoto Protocol enters force
key_developments.description: Kyoto Protocol entered force 16 February 2005 — formal foundational Kyoto Protocol framework operational.

key_developments.title: Paris Agreement adopted COP21
key_developments.description: Paris Agreement adopted 12 December 2015 in Paris France at COP21 — formal foundational Paris Agreement bottom-up-NDCs framework adopted.

key_developments.title: Paris Agreement enters force + Marrakech CMA1
key_developments.description: Paris Agreement entered force 4 November 2016 + COP22 + CMA1 held 7-18 November 2016 in Marrakech Morocco — formal foundational Paris Agreement framework operational.

key_developments.title: Trump-administration Paris Agreement withdrawal announcement
key_developments.description: Trump-administration Paris Agreement withdrawal announcement 1 June 2017 — formal Trump-first-Paris-withdrawal-announcement framework operational.

key_developments.title: Trump-administration Paris Agreement withdrawal effective
key_developments.description: Trump-administration Paris Agreement withdrawal effective 4 November 2020 — formal Trump-first-Paris-withdrawal-effective framework operational.

key_developments.title: Biden-administration Paris Agreement rejoining + COP26 Glasgow
key_developments.description: Biden-administration Paris Agreement rejoining 19 February 2021 + COP26 held 31 October - 12 November 2021 in Glasgow UK + Glasgow Climate Pact + Just Transition + Mitigation Work Programme framework — formal Biden-Paris-rejoining + COP26 Glasgow framework operational.

key_developments.title: COP27 Sharm El-Sheikh + Loss and Damage Fund established
key_developments.description: COP27 held 6-20 November 2022 in Sharm El-Sheikh Egypt + Loss and Damage Fund established — formal foundational Loss and Damage Fund framework adopted.

key_developments.title: COP28 Dubai + Global Stocktake + Loss and Damage Fund operationalized + transitioning-away-from-fossil-fuels
key_developments.description: COP28 held 30 November - 13 December 2023 in Dubai UAE + first Global Stocktake adopted + Loss and Damage Fund operationalized + UAE Consensus calling for transitioning-away-from-fossil-fuels framework — formal foundational COP28 + first-Global-Stocktake + Loss-and-Damage-Fund-operational + transitioning-away-from-fossil-fuels framework operational.

key_developments.title: COP29 Baku + NCQG USD 300 billion
key_developments.description: COP29 held 11-22 November 2024 in Baku Azerbaijan + NCQG New Collective Quantified Goal USD 300 billion annual climate-finance target framework adopted — formal NCQG framework operational.

key_developments.title: Trump-administration second Paris Agreement withdrawal + COP30 Belém + ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion
key_developments.description: Trump-administration second Paris Agreement withdrawal effective 27 January 2025 + COP30 held 10-21 November 2025 in Belém Brazil + ICJ Climate Change Advisory Opinion 22 October 2025 framework — formal Trump-second-Paris-withdrawal + COP30 Belém + ICJ-Climate-Advisory-Opinion framework operational.

structural_findings: UNFCCC operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places UNFCCC in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-climate-change framework + foundational climate-change framework alongside Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-climate-change framework + foundational climate-change framework alongside Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Common But Differentiated Responsibilities CBDR principle framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal NDCs framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Trump-administration-Paris-Agreement-withdrawal oscillation 2017-2021-2025 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal COP1 1995 Berlin to COP30 2025 Belém Brazil framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Global Stocktake 2023 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Loss and Damage Fund 2022-2023 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Just Transition Work Programme framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Mitigation Work Programme framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CARICOM-led ICJ Climate Change Advisory Opinion 2025 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Paris Agreement 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal NDCs framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal NDCs framework with formal bottom-up self-determined-contributions framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Loss and Damage Fund + Climate Finance framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Loss and Damage Fund framework.
198 UNFCCC member state + 195 Paris Agreement member state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive universal coverage framework.

1988 IPCC + 1992 UNFCCC + 1994 entry into force + 1995 COP1 + 1997 Kyoto + 2005 Kyoto entry into force + 2015 Paris + 2016 entry into force + 2017 Trump-withdrawal + 2020 effective + 2021 Biden-rejoining + COP26 + 2022 COP27 + Loss and Damage + 2023 COP28 + Global Stocktake + Loss-and-Damage-Fund-operational + transitioning-away-from-fossil-fuels + 2024 COP29 + NCQG + 2025 Trump-second-withdrawal + COP30 Belém + ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion UNFCCC-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

UNFCCC + Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement + NDCs + Loss and Damage Fund + Global Stocktake + Just Transition Work Programme + Mitigation Work Programme + ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion formal nonet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal cross-border-climate-displacement-of-families framework.
editorial_notes: UNFCCC + Paris Agreement jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global international-climate-change foundational-UNFCCC-treaty meta-framework (UNFCCC 1992/1994 + Kyoto Protocol 1997/2005 + Paris Agreement 2015/2016 + COP1 1995 Berlin to COP30 2025 Belém + Loss and Damage Fund 2022-2023 + Global Stocktake 2023 + Just Transition + Mitigation Work Programmes + NCQG USD 300 billion 2024 + Trump-withdrawal oscillation 2017-2021-2025 + Biden-rejoining 2021 + ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion 2025 + IPCC 1988 predecessor). Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-climate-change framework + foundational climate-change framework alongside Kyoto Protocol + Paris Agreement + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CBDR principle + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal NDCs + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Trump-Paris-withdrawal oscillation 2017-2021-2025 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal COP1 to COP30 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Global Stocktake 2023 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Loss and Damage Fund 2022-2023 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Just Transition + Mitigation Work Programmes + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal CARICOM-led ICJ Climate Change Advisory Opinion 22 October 2025 + central jurisdiction of formal 1.5°C-2°C temperature-goal + central jurisdiction of formal NDCs bottom-up + central jurisdiction of formal Loss and Damage Fund + Climate Finance NCQG USD 300 billion 2024 Baku + 198 UNFCCC member states + 195 Paris Agreement member states + UNFCCC Secretariat Bonn + COP30 Belém Brazil 2025 + 1988 IPCC + 1992 UNFCCC + 1994 entry into force + 1995 COP1 + 1997 Kyoto + 2005 Kyoto entry into force + 2015 Paris + 2016 entry into force + 2017 Trump + 2020 effective + 2021 Biden + COP26 + 2022 COP27 + Loss and Damage + 2023 COP28 + Global Stocktake + transitioning-away-from-fossil-fuels + 2024 COP29 + NCQG + 2025 Trump-second-withdrawal + COP30 Belém + ICJ Climate Advisory Opinion UNFCCC-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: UNFCCC + Paris Agreement Just Transition framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal cross-border-climate-displacement-of-families framework.
